method: A merge-intervals function with a real bug (does not merge touching intervals) + a real Node test covering that case. The test FAILS; one OpenRouter call is asked to return the corrected file; the file is applied; the REAL test command is the verdict. One call — not wired into evolve (which would cost one call per variant per generation).
significance: Demonstrates the production evaluation path end-to-end — real model + real failing test + real test verdict — for ~$0.0005, reusing the real-test-as-oracle approach. Bridges Tier-2 (real surface code on synthetic tasks, ADR-106) toward real-LLM-on-real-SWE-tasks (ADR-098).
